message:
fix: TUI scroll safety, query robustness, and truncation zero-copy

Analytics and explorer detail panes now track last_detail_max_scroll via
Cell to prevent scrolling past content bounds — eliminates blank-screen
state when rapidly scrolling long insight cards or message details.

- queries.rs: Switch fetch_unacked_for_agent from get_named() to
  positional get_as() for resilience against column name changes
- query_integration.rs: Fix block_on helper to use runtime-backed Cx
  via rt.handle().spawn() instead of Cx::for_testing() which lacks
  io_driver/timer_driver backing (root cause of test hangs)
- tui_widgets.rs: Return Cow<str> from truncate_width to avoid
  unnecessary String allocation when input fits within max_width
- contacts.rs: Fix integer overflow in scaled_level range calculation
  (i64::from(max - min) could overflow when min is negative)
- dashboard.rs: Adapt truncate() return type to match Cow signature
- messages.rs: Call .into_owned() on Cow return from truncate_width
- build_slots.rs: Remove incorrect `if t > 0` guard on TTL matching —
  clamp(60, ...) already handles the lower bound correctly, and the
  guard silently fell through to the default for explicit zero values

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -860,7 +860,7 @@ {"id":"br-2ei.9.6","title":"E2E: HTTP server parity suite (auth/rate-limit/streamable/mail/CORS/logging)","description":"Add a single HTTP-focused E2E script that exercises the *user-visible* HTTP server surface end-to-end, using the shared harness (br-2ei.9.1). Script: - scripts/e2e_http.sh Coverage (must be parity-oriented, not "best-effort"): 1. Health + well-known endpoints: /health/* and /.well-known/* payloads + status codes. 2. Bearer auth: - Authorization required when configured - OPTIONS + /health bypass - constant-time compare (behavioral checks) 3. JWT auth: - HS256 and JWKS modes (use deterministic test vectors) - aud/iss validation - role claim parsing - failure semantics always 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"} 4. Rate limiting: - Redis backend allow/deny sequences - Redis failure -> memory fallback - identity derivation (sub vs host) 5. Peer addr + localhost bypass: - localhost allowed only when NO forwarded headers - IPv4/IPv6/IPv4-mapped IPv6 cases 6. Streamable HTTP MCP: - Accept/Content-Type normalization - base path /api and /api/ semantics - passthrough behavior - localhost Authorization injection when configured 7. /mail SSR UI: - fetch key pages and assert DOM markers - sanitize XSS payloads 8. CORS: - OPTIONS preflight headers - default allow-* semantics 9. Request logging + OTEL: - logs emitted only when enabled - OTEL misconfig doesn't crash 10. Tool filtering profiles (context reduction): - With filtering disabled: tool list matches full baseline. - With filtering enabled (minimal + one custom include/exclude case): - `tools/list` is filtered - `resource://tooling/directory` reflects filtered tools/clusters 11. Instrumentation (query tracking): - With instrumentation enabled, at least one DB-backed tool call emits a `tool_query_stats` log entry. 12. ACK TTL background worker (+ optional escalation): - With ACK_TTL enabled (ttl=0), create an ack_required message and assert logs contain `ack_overdue`. - With escalation mode file_reservation, assert a reservation is created (resource read or DB query) without crashing. Artifacts/logging: - tests/artifacts/http// - For each case: record request (curl args), response status, headers, body, and expected-vs-actual. - When applicable, include server stdout/stderr logs and config env snapshot (redacting secrets). ## Acceptance Criteria 1. scripts/e2e_http.sh exists and can be run via scripts/e2e_test.sh http. 2. Script exits non-zero on first mismatch and prints expected vs actual. 3. Artifacts include per-case HTTP transcripts + server logs. 4. Script is deterministic (no random ports without recording, stable vectors). 5. Tool filtering checks cover at least: disabled baseline + minimal profile + one custom include/exclude case. 6. Instrumentation check asserts presence of `tool_query_stats` in captured server logs. 7. ACK TTL check asserts presence of `ack_overdue` (and file_reservation escalation when enabled).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralDog","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:22.826465268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:38:21.198477689Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:38:21.198452152Z","close_reason":"Implemented unified HTTP E2E suite (scripts/e2e_http.sh + tests/e2e/test_http.sh). Fixed mail UI inbox rendering to match templates and corrected mail_ui XSS assertions. Verified: AM_E2E_KEEP_TMP=1 ./scripts/e2e_test.sh http (pass).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"br-2ei.9.7","title":"E2E: LLM llm_mode smoke (stubbed)","description":"E2E: LLM llm_mode smoke (stubbed, offline). Scope: - Start server with LLM enabled and stubbed completion client. - Exercise `summarize_thread` with llm_mode=true and `macro_prepare_thread` llm_mode path. - Validate refined summary fields and error fallback when stub returns invalid JSON. Logging/artifacts: - Capture HTTP transcripts + tool payloads. - Store artifacts under tests/artifacts/llm//. - Emit expected vs actual JSON diffs on mismatch. Dependencies: - br-2ei.9.1 (E2E harness) - br-2ei.13.2 (LLM impl) - br-2ei.13.3 (LLM tests/spec) ## Acceptance Criteria 1. E2E test uses a deterministic stubbed LLM client and runs via `scripts/e2e_test.sh` (llm path). 2. `summarize_thread` with llm_mode=true returns refined fields that match expected JSON fixtures. 3. `macro_prepare_thread` with llm_mode=true exercises the LLM path and returns refined summaries. 4. Invalid JSON from the stub triggers the legacy fallback behavior (non-LLM summary + logged error) and is asserted. 5. Artifacts (requests/responses + diffs) are written to `tests/artifacts/llm//`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:15:12.039153780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:03:06.967900215Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:03:06.967878724Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"br-2ei.9.8","title":"E2E: Notifications signal files","description":"E2E: Notifications (signal files) parity. Scope: - Start server with notifications enabled. - send_message to multiple recipients (to/cc/bcc) and assert signal files for to+cc only. - fetch_inbox clears signal for that agent; verify pending list updates. - debounce behavior: repeated sends within window do not spam signals (per legacy spec). Logging/artifacts: - Capture filesystem snapshots of signals dir before/after. - Store artifacts under tests/artifacts/notifications//. - Emit expected vs actual file lists + JSON diffs on mismatch. Dependencies: - br-2ei.9.1 (E2E harness) - br-2ei.12.2 (Notifications impl) - br-2ei.12.3 (Notifications tests/spec) ## Acceptance Criteria 1. E2E test runs via `scripts/e2e_test.sh` notifications path and uses temp dirs (no destructive commands). 2. After send_message, signals are emitted for `to` + `cc` recipients only; `bcc` produces none. 3. fetch_inbox clears the signal for that agent and the pending list is updated accordingly. 4. Debounce behavior is verified: repeated sends within the debounce window do not create duplicate signal entries (assert via file list + timestamps or sequence numbers). 5. Failures emit clear diffs and artifacts are written to `tests/artifacts/notifications//`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:15:49.568178405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:03:06.969373873Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:03:06.969354547Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.12.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.12.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"br-2em1l","title":"query_integration tests hang: Cx::for_testing() lacks runtime backing","description":"ALL 44 tests in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/query_integration.rs hang indefinitely. Root cause: block_on() creates RuntimeBuilder::current_thread() + Cx::for_testing(), but Cx::for_testing() returns a stub context with no io_driver/timer_driver/blocking_pool. Async pool ops need runtime backing, so futures return Pending forever. Fix: use rt.handle().spawn() or create a testing block_on helper that wires Cx to the runtime.","notes":"2026-03-04 BlueCrane: Investigated and patched crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/query_integration.rs block_on helper to use RuntimeBuilder::current_thread + rt.handle().spawn + Cx::current() (runtime-backed Cx) instead of Cx::for_testing(). Verification blocked: rch exec cargo test fails before tests run due unrelated compile error in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs (E0599 self.pool.clear_idle() method not found on Arc>).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlueCrane","created_at":"2026-03-04T20:55:28.566704577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:48:03.319516075Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","infrastructure","test"]} +{"id":"br-2em1l","title":"query_integration tests hang: Cx::for_testing() lacks runtime backing","description":"ALL 44 tests in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/query_integration.rs hang indefinitely. Root cause: block_on() creates RuntimeBuilder::current_thread() + Cx::for_testing(), but Cx::for_testing() returns a stub context with no io_driver/timer_driver/blocking_pool. Async pool ops need runtime backing, so futures return Pending forever. Fix: use rt.handle().spawn() or create a testing block_on helper that wires Cx to the runtime.","notes":"2026-03-04 BlueCrane: Investigated and patched crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/query_integration.rs block_on helper to use RuntimeBuilder::current_thread + rt.handle().spawn + Cx::current() (runtime-backed Cx) instead of Cx::for_testing(). Verification blocked: rch exec cargo test fails before tests run due unrelated compile error in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs (E0599 self.pool.clear_idle() method not found on Arc>).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlueCrane","created_at":"2026-03-04T20:55:28.566704577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:13:22.978006262Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","infrastructure","test"],"comments":[{"id":1204,"issue_id":"br-2em1l","author":"IcyCondor","text":"Infra mitigation applied 2026-03-04: routed CPU-heavy runs with explicit TMPDIR/CARGO_TARGET_DIR off /tmp and quarantined flaky worker vmi1227854 (status set to draining) after repeated remote exit 127 incidents. Continuing verification on healthy workers via rch.","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:13:22Z"}]} {"id":"br-2f1mx","title":"T7.2: Implement HTML/SVG/Text screen export","description":"Add screen export capabilities using frankentui's export module. Operators can save the current screen state to a file for sharing or documentation. KEYBINDINGS: - Ctrl+E: Export menu (choose format) - 'h': Export as HTML - 's': Export as SVG - 't': Export as plain text EXPORT BEHAVIOR: - Captures current frame buffer - Applies theme colors in HTML/SVG output - Saves to $AM_EXPORT_DIR (default: ~/.mcp_agent_mail/exports/) - Filename: am_export_{screen}_{timestamp}.{ext} - Toast: "Exported to {path}" HTML EXPORT: ```html
``` FILES: tui_app.rs, new export helper","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria: - [ ] Ctrl+E opens export format menu - [ ] HTML export preserves colors and layout - [ ] SVG export renders as vector graphics - [ ] Text export strips ANSI codes - [ ] Files saved with timestamp-based names - [ ] Export directory configurable via env var Plan-space hardening additions: - [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope - [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead - [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHare","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-16T20:43:28.153570886Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T20:43:28.153550788Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2f1mx","depends_on_id":"br-72en9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"br-2fc0","title":"T3.7: Tests for bench runner, statistics, and baseline comparison","description":"## Objective Protect benchmark correctness with unit and integration tests across measurement, aggregation, and baseline comparison behavior. ## Work - Add tests for statistical computations and edge-case stability. - Validate baseline compare logic, threshold decisions, and regression reporting semantics. - Assert deterministic output contracts for automation consumers. ## Deliverable A comprehensive benchmark test layer that prevents subtle performance-analysis regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria - Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions). - Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction. - Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step. - CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking. - Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","notes":"Added bench coverage hardening tests in CLI crate: (1) crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs now asserts fixture signature determinism against a known SHA-256 prefix, verifies linear-sequence statistics (mean/median/p95/p99/stddev/variance), covers single-sample and identical-sample stability, and checks 9% vs 11% baseline threshold behavior at a 10% regression threshold. (2) Existing bench.rs tests already cover empty-sample rejection, baseline save/load roundtrip, baseline compare/apply, timed-run contracts, and DB seed fixture idempotence/reseed behavior. (3) crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs now includes integration-style handler tests for handle_bench list/json output contracts and quick profile defaults (warmup=1/runs=3) plus report artifact creation under a temporary CWD. Validation attempted: cargo fmt -p mcp-agent-mail-cli -- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs passed. Targeted cargo test invocations are currently blocked by existing compile failure in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_app.rs:445 (tracked in br-3vwi.12.3.4).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealBrook","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:48.455279402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:34:21.472531257Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T03:34:21.472511069Z","close_reason":"Bench test coverage implemented per scope; runtime validation is currently gated by known server compile blocker br-3vwi.12.3.4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2fc0","depends_on_id":"br-71g9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":409,"issue_id":"br-2fc0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T3.7: Tests for Bench Runner, Statistics, and Baseline Comparison ## What to test ### Unit tests 1. **Statistics correctness**: Known input samples → verify mean, stddev, p95, p99 Examples: [1,2,3,4,5] → mean=3, median=3, p95=5, p99=5 [10,20,30,...,100] → verify all percentiles 2. **Edge cases**: Empty samples, single sample, all identical values 3. **Fixture signature**: Verify SHA-256 matches Python script output for same inputs 4. **Baseline save/load roundtrip**: Save, reload, verify equality 5. **Baseline comparison**: Known current + baseline → verify delta and regression flag 6. **Regression threshold**: 9% delta with 10% threshold = ok; 11% = regression ### Integration tests 7. **DB seeding**: Verify seed_bench_db creates correct number of messages (50 + 10 = 60 messages, 2 agents, 1 project) 8. **CLI flag parsing**: Verify all flags are accepted 9. **--list flag**: Verify it prints benchmark names without running them 10. **Quick mode**: Verify --quick uses warmup=1, runs=3 ### Behavioral equivalence test 11. **Schema compatibility**: Verify JSON output from `am bench --json` has the same schema_version, hardware, and benchmarks structure as bench_cli.sh's summary JSON ## Location crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs (mod tests) crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/tests/bench_integration.rs ","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:31:07Z"}]} {"id":"br-2fjv","title":"Fix misleading subcommands in mcp-agent-mail server binary","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:59:26.979357810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:59:32.769166348Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:59:32.769147563Z","close_reason":"Removed stub subcommands from server binary; updated CLI guidance and docs; ran fmt/clippy/tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
